grades.note: No registry entries exist for this topic; the claim (including the '7,700 unfilled posts' and 'halved graduate pipeline' figures) is treated as non-registry assertion and requires primary-source citation.
Poland 1999 reversal and the absence of statutory guarantees for transfers are not supported by any curated registry facts; claim stands as non-registry assertion.
The Gersekarát single-case funding collapse is not present in the registry; fragility inference is a non-registry claim needing primary documentation.
Documented precedents cited (Hódmezővásárhely 2023, 2018 bus network) are not in the registry; mitigation-measures enforcement claim is not registry-backed.
The summary of international evidence on disproportionate harms and depopulation is not supported by entries in the curated registry and is treated as non-registry evidence.
Conditional claim that S4 lacks mandatory pre-transfer verification is not recorded in the registry; the assertion is non-registry-backed.
Claim that excluding non-state maintainers in receiving schools forecloses pluralism and accelerates depopulation is not supported by registry facts and remains a non-registry assertion.

Proposal for a national evidence infrastructure and the stated absence of a Hungary-specific causal study are not captured in the registry (registry currently empty); treated as non-registry claim about a documented data gap.
The quoted transport cost range (800–1,500 EUR/pupil/year) and net fiscal-benefit calculation are not verified in the registry and are thus non-registry assertions.
Risk to rural teaching posts and redeployment without binding guarantees is not supported by registry entries and is treated as a non-registry claim.
The Polish ~70-pupil viability threshold and its transferability to Hungary are not documented in the registry; this fact remains unverified by the curated source base.
The stated 7,700-post shortfall and halved graduate pipeline and the projected causal link to future digital-workforce deficits are not in the registry and are treated as non-registry assertions.
